skill.name: ai-seo
skill.description: When the user wants to optimize content for AI search engines, get cited by LLMs, or appear in AI-generated answers. Also use when the user mentions 'AI SEO,' 'AEO,' 'GEO,' 'LLMO,' 'answer engine optimization,' 'generative engine optimization,' 'LLM optimization,' 'AI Overviews,' 'optimize for ChatGPT,' 'optimize for Perplexity,' 'AI citations,' 'AI visibility,' 'zero-click search,' 'how do I show up in AI answers,' 'LLM mentions,' or 'optimize for Claude/Gemini.' Use this whenever someone wants their content to be cited or surfaced by AI assistants and AI search engines. For traditional technical and on-page SEO audits, see seo-audit. For structured data implementation, see schema-markup.
skill.summary: Optimize content to get cited by AI search engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: All 87 static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate AI SEO marketing skill. The static scanner incorrectly flagged markdown code blocks (backticks) as shell commands, markdown links with '../' as path traversal, and text content as cryptographic algorithms. No actual security risks were identified after manual review of the skill files.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
high_findings.description: Static scanner incorrectly flagged text content as cryptographic code. The evals.json file contains evaluation prompts with no cryptographic implementation.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain evaluation test prompts with no cryptographic code - text mentioning algorithms is not the same as cryptographic implementation

high_findings.title: False Positive: Path Traversal Sequence
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged markdown link '../' as path traversal. The skill uses standard markdown relative links.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line contains markdown link with relative path '../../tools/REGISTRY.md' - standard documentation reference, not file system access

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged markdown code block delimiters as shell commands. The skill uses standard markdown code blocks for content examples.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backticks are markdown code block delimiters (```), not shell backtick execution. This is a documentation skill with example content templates

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Deprecated With Statement
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged regular English word 'with' as Python deprecated statement.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line contains 'with' as English preposition in sentence about schema markup, not Python code

low_findings.title: False Positive: System/Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged content about analyzing AI search platforms as reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Content discusses analyzing AI search platforms for marketing purposes, not technical network reconnaissance

content.user_title: Get Your Content Cited by AI Search
content.value_statement: Traditional SEO gets you ranked, but AI SEO gets you cited. This skill helps you optimize content so AI systems like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews cite your brand as a source.
actual_capabilities: Run AI visibility audits across ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, and other AI platforms
Apply the three pillars framework: Structure (extractable content), Authority (citation-worthy), Presence (third-party mentions)
Create content patterns optimized for AI extraction including definition blocks, comparison tables, and FAQ sections
Check and configure robots.txt for AI bot access (GPTBot, PerplexityBot, ClaudeBot)
Implement schema markup for AI content understanding
Monitor AI citation share using tools like Otterly AI, Peec AI, and ZipTie
limitations: Does not directly modify website code or robots.txt files
Cannot guarantee citation by AI systems - only optimizes for probability
Does not handle traditional technical SEO (crawlability, site speed, etc.) - use seo-audit skill for that
Results depend on AI platform decisions which are not fully transparent

use_cases.title: SaaS Product Visibility
use_cases.description: A SaaS company wants to appear in AI recommendations when prospects ask 'best project management software' or 'what is [category] tool'.

use_cases.title: Content Strategy Planning
use_cases.description: A content marketer wants to understand which content formats get cited most by AI systems to prioritize content creation.

use_cases.title: Competitive AI Analysis
use_cases.description: A business wants to understand why competitors are getting cited by AI but they are not, and what changes are needed.
use_cases.target_user: Digital marketing agencies and brand managers

prompt_templates.title: Basic AI Visibility Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Check if our brand [brand name] appears in AI-generated answers for queries like '[key query 1]', '[key query 2]', '[key query 3]'. Run an AI visibility audit across Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, and Perplexity.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick assessment of current AI search presence

prompt_templates.title: Content Optimization Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Optimize our blog post at [URL] for AI citation. Apply the three pillars framework. Check content extractability, add structure for AI extraction, and suggest authority signals to add.
prompt_templates.scenario: Optimizing existing content for AI search

prompt_templates.title: AI Bot Access Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Review our robots.txt and check if AI crawlers (GPTBot, PerplexityBot, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended) are blocked. Provide recommended configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive AI SEO Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comprehensive AI SEO strategy for our [industry] business. Include: 1) Current AI visibility audit, 2) Content optimization recommendations using the three pillars, 3) Third-party presence strategy (Wikipedia, Reddit, review sites), 4) Schema markup implementation plan, 5) Monthly monitoring setup.

output_examples.input: How do I show up in ChatGPT recommendations for 'best CRM software'?
output: AI Visibility Audit Results:
1. PLATFORM CHECK
- ChatGPT: Search for your brand in 'best CRM' queries
- Perplexity: Check if you appear as cited source
- Google AI Overviews: Test in Google search
2. THREE PILLARS ASSESSMENT
Structure: Add clear definition block, comparison tables, FAQ section
Authority: Add statistics (+37% boost), expert quotes (+30%), citations (+40%)
Presence: Get Wikipedia mention, Reddit discussion, G2/TrustRadius reviews
3. CONTENT PRIORITIES
- Comparison articles (33% of AI citations)
- Definitive guides (15% of citations)
- Original research with citable statistics

output_examples.input: Should we block GPTBot in robots.txt?
output: Tradeoff Analysis:
BLOCKING AI BOTS:
- Prevents content from being used in AI training
- Protects content from being cited without direct traffic
ALLOWING AI BOTS:
- Enables citation in AI answers (potential brand exposure)
- ~45% of Google searches show AI Overviews
- AI citations drive awareness even without clicks
RECOMMENDED CONFIGURATION:
Allow: GPTBot, ChatGPT-User, PerplexityBot, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended, Bingbot
Consider blocking: CCBot (Common Crawl - training only)

best_practices: Lead every section with a direct answer - AI extracts the first clear statement, not buried conclusions
Include specific statistics with cited sources - Princeton GEO research shows +40% visibility boost from citations
Build third-party presence (Wikipedia, Reddit, reviews) - brands are 6.5x more likely to be cited via third-party sources than their own domains
anti_patterns: Writing for AI instead of humans - content that reads like it was written to game algorithms won't get cited or convert
Keyword stuffing - actively reduces AI visibility by 10% according to Princeton GEO research
Gating all content behind paywalls - AI can't access gated content, keeping authoritative content open increases citation probability

faq.question: What is the difference between AI SEO and traditional SEO?
faq.answer: Traditional SEO gets you ranked on search results pages. AI SEO gets you cited as a source in AI-generated answers. While traditional SEO requires ranking on page 1, AI systems can cite well-structured content from page 2 or 3 if it provides clear, extractable answers.

faq.question: Which AI platforms should I optimize for?
faq.answer: Prioritize based on your audience: Google AI Overviews (45% of Google searches), ChatGPT with search (broad reach), Perplexity (highly cites sources), Claude (uses Brave Search). Each has different ranking factors but all value structure, authority, and citations.

faq.question: How long does it take to see results from AI SEO?
faq.answer: AI platforms index continuously. Structural improvements can show results within 2-4 weeks for featured snippets. Full AI citation impact typically takes 1-3 months of consistent optimization and monitoring.

faq.question: Does blocking AI bots protect my content?
faq.answer: Blocking AI bots prevents training and citation but doesn't stop all AI access. Major platforms respect robots.txt but some crawlers may not. Blocking reduces AI visibility significantly - this is a business decision between content protection and AI exposure.

faq.question: What content types get cited most by AI?
faq.answer: Comparison articles (~33% of citations), definitive guides (~15%), original research (~12%), best-of listicles (~10%), and product pages (~10%). Structured, authoritative content with clear definitions and cited statistics performs best.

faq.question: How do I measure AI SEO success?
faq.answer: Use tools like Otterly AI, Peec AI, or ZipTie to track citation share. Manual monthly checks work for smaller scale - test top 20 queries across platforms and track if you're cited. Monitor referral traffic from AI sources in Google Analytics.
